protocol: Core Restore 14-day liver cleanse (Designs for Health)

dates.longitudinal_extension_pending: Garmin export will extend window back to first wearable-tracked detox cycle

phases.label: Long-baseline 2025
phases.notes: Pre-2025-detox baseline. Populated from Garmin data (no WHOOP yet). Compare resting HR, body battery, sleep duration to subsequent phases.

phases.label: Core Restore (7d, Jun 2025)
phases.notes: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER DATES — Trisha to confirm exact start. SAME Core Restore protocol as Apr 2026, but only 7 days duration (half the standard cycle). This makes the 2025 vs 2026 comparison a within-subject dose-response observation: did doubling the duration produce a proportional effect, a plateau, or a different effect altogether? Garmin metrics only (no WHOOP at this time).

phases.label: Between-detoxes window
phases.notes: 10-month observation window between the 7-day and 14-day cycles. Useful for detecting whether the 7-day cycle produced durable effects on resting HR / body battery / sleep, or whether the metrics reverted to long-baseline. The longer this window persists at improved values, the more durable the protocol effect. Garmin throughout; WHOOP joins partway through (purchase date TBD by Trisha — the date the WHOOP CSV starts is the seam).

phases.label: Pre-detox baseline (Apr 2026)
phases.notes: Immediate 27-day pre-detox baseline for the Apr 2026 Core Restore. WHOOP data only (clean WHOOP-only window for this analysis).

phases.label: Core Restore (14d, Apr 2026)
phases.notes: 14 consecutive days of Core Restore protocol: AM + PM shake, elimination diet (no gluten, dairy, soy, corn, eggs, sugar, alcohol, caffeine), Designs for Health supplement stack. SAME protocol as Jun 2025 7-day cycle but doubled duration. Cross-phase comparison hypothesis: does duration scale linearly with HRV / RHR shift, or does the second week deliver diminishing returns?

phases.label: Post-detox clean
phases.notes: Single rest day immediately following the 14-day cycle.
